   409→            ;;

   412→            ;;

   415→            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, custom"
   416→            return 1
   417→            ;;
   418→    esac
   419→
   420→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   421→    local json
   422→    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
   423→
   424→    # Validate JSON
   425→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   426→        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
   427→        return 1
   428→    fi
   429→
   430→    echo "$json"

   433→# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
   434→# Args:
   435→#   $1 - LLM output text
   436→# Returns:
   437→#   Extracted JSON
   438→llm_extract_json() {
   439→    local output="$1"
   440→
   441→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   442→    local extracted
   443→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

   445→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   446→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then

   448→        return 0
   449→    fi
   450→

   453→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then

   455→    else
   456→        echo "$output"
   457→    fi
   458→}

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================

  --setting-sources <sources>                       Comma-separated list of setting sources to load (user, project, local).
  --plugin-dir <paths...>                           Load plugins from directories for this session only (repeatable)
  -v, --version                                     Output the version number
  -h, --help                                        Display help for command

Commands:

# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    echo "$output"
}

_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {

    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=claude-cli, model=$model, wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-unknown}ms, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=claude-cli, model=$model, wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-unknown}ms, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=claude-cli, model=$model, wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-unknown}ms, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenAI API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=openai-api, model=$model, wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms, cost=\$unknown, tokens=${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================

#!/bin/bash
# Integration test for session-start handler
# Tests the complete session-start workflow including all features

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Track test results
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Get script directory
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Test helper functions
log_test() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}TEST:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
}

pass() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ PASS:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

fail() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ FAIL:${NC} $1"
    TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    log_test "Setting up test environment"

    # Create temporary test directory
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t sidekick-test-XXXXXX)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"

    # Create sidekick structure in test directory
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions"

    # Copy sidekick files to test directory
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/sidekick.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/lib/"
    cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/config.defaults" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/"

    # Copy handlers if they exist
    if [ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" ]; then
        cp "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/handlers/session-start.sh" "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/handlers/"
    fi

    # Copy features if they exist
    if [ -d "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features" ]; then
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/"*.sh "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/" 2>/dev/null || true
        cp -r "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/features/prompts/"* "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/features/prompts/" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Make sidekick.sh executable
    chmod +x "$TEST_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.sh"

    # Create mock Claude CLI
    MOCK_CLAUDE="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
    cat > "$MOCK_CLAUDE" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Mock Claude CLI for testing
# Returns minimal valid JSON output

# Check if we're being called for resume generation
# (will have prompt containing "CURRENT SESSION TOPIC")
if grep -q "CURRENT SESSION TOPIC" <<< "$@" 2>/dev/null; then
    cat <<'JSON'
```json
{

  "resume_last_goal_message": "Shall we resume testing?",
  "last_objective_in_progress": "Navigate the test matrix",

}
```

    15→#   - plugin.sh: Plugin discovery and dependency resolution
    16→#
    17→# This modular structure keeps each namespace focused and testable while

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library loader for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file sources all namespace libraries in dependency order.
# Each namespace is in a separate file for better organization:
#
#   - logging.sh: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - json.sh: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - utils.sh: General-purpose helpers
#   - paths.sh: Scope detection and path management
#   - config.sh: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - process.sh: Background process lifecycle
#   - workspace.sh: Temporary workspace creation
#   - llm.sh: Pluggable LLM providers with isolation
#   - plugin.sh: Plugin discovery and dependency resolution
#
# This modular structure keeps each namespace focused and testable while
# maintaining backward compatibility with code that sources common.sh.

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Get the directory where this script lives
_COMMON_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Strict mode (applied to all sourced files)
set -euo pipefail

# Source all namespace libraries in dependency order
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/logging.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/json.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/utils.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/paths.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/config.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/process.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/workspace.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/llm.sh"
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${_COMMON_DIR}/plugin.sh"

# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl

# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
_llm_log_completion() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local wall_time_ms="$3"

    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    fi

    # Build token component
    local tokens="unknown"
    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    fi

    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
}
